question: How can I list all queues with message details?
answer: rabbitmqadmin list queues name messages_ready messages_unacknowledged

question: How do I list all exchanges in RabbitMQ?
How can I see bindings for a specific exchange?
answer: rabbitmqadmin list bindings source_name destination_name routing_key

question: How do I check the cluster status?
How can I view the RabbitMQ node status?
answer: rabbitmqadmin status <node-name>  // <node-name> is the name of the node

question: How do I list all active connections to RabbitMQ?
How can I view detailed information about channels?
How do I monitor message statistics for a specific queue?
answer: rabbitmqadmin list queues name messages_publish_rate messages_deliver_rate

question: How can I list all RabbitMQ users?
How do I view permissions for RabbitMQ users?
How can I see all defined RabbitMQ policies?
How do I peek into a specific queue to see messages?
How can I delete a problematic queue?
How do I run diagnostics on a RabbitMQ node?
How can I check if alarms are raised on a RabbitMQ node?
answer: There isn't a direct rabbitmqadmin command.  Use the HTTP API directly for alarms.

question: How do I check the health of a RabbitMQ node?
answer: rabbitmqadmin declare policy name=node_health_check pattern='' definition='{\ha-mode\": \"all\"

question: How can I check memory usage on the RabbitMQ node?
How do I reset a RabbitMQ node?
answer: There is no direct equivalent. Resetting requires stopping and restarting the node.

question: How can I stop a RabbitMQ application on a node?
answer: rabbitmqadmin stop_app <node-name> // <node-name> is the name of the node

question: How do I start a RabbitMQ application on a node?
answer: rabbitmqadmin start_app <node-name> // <node-name> is the name of the node

question: How can I forcefully disconnect a specific connection?
How do I list all queues with their consumer counts?
How can I add a user to RabbitMQ?
answer: rabbitmqadmin declare user name=<username> password=<password> tags=<tags>
